name: Cauliflower Risotto with Saffron
recipeIngredient: 9 to 12 cups of light, boiling chicken broth
4 cups small cauliflower florets (each the width of a thumbnail)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 2 ounces butter over medium heat in a large, heavy stockpot.
2. Add the chopped onion and saute until the onion is soft and golden, stirring occasionally for about 7 minutes.
3. Add the Arborio rice and stir well to coat the rice with the butter. Sprinkle with the saffron threads and cook for one minute, stirring.
4. Turn the heat to medium-high. Gradually add 2 cups of the chicken broth (or enough to just cover the rice), stirring continuously. When most of the broth has been absorbed, add the cauliflower and stir well. Continue adding boiling broth in increments until all is absorbed and the rice is al dente.
5. Add approximately 1/2 cup more boiling broth, stirring until absorbed. Repeat this process as needed with the remaining stock, ensuring a total of 9 to 12 cups are used.
6. Stir in the remaining butter into the rice, along with the freshly grated Parmigiano-Reggiano. Adjust texture with additional broth if necessary.
7. Taste for seasoning and serve hot from a large platter onto warmed plates.
Yield: 4 servings
